## What

<!-- A short, single-sentence description of the change. -->

## Why

<!-- The problem this PR solves, or the capability it adds. -->

## Type of change

Pick one:

- [ ] `feat:` — new capability visible to users, operators, or downstream tools
- [ ] `fix:` — bug fix
- [ ] `perf:` — performance change with no behaviour change
- [ ] `refactor:` — internal change with no new behaviour
- [ ] `docs:` — documentation only
- [ ] `test:` — tests only
- [ ] `chore:` — tooling, CI, dependencies, or housekeeping
- [ ] `build:` — build system or release artefacts

Breaking changes must add `!` after the type and be called out under
**Breaking change** below.

## Verification

<!-- How you tested the change. For a feature, link the run, fixtures, or
golden task. For a fix, link the regression test. For docs, link the
validation you ran. -->

## Breaking change

<!-- If `feat!:` or `fix!:` etc., describe the migration path for users. -->

## Documentation

<!-- Which docs moved, were added, or were removed. PRD, PHASES, README,
schemas, or skill notes all count. -->

## Risk and rollback

<!-- What can go wrong. How do we detect it. How do we roll back. -->
